page: Microsoft Azure E16-4ads_v7 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the Eadsv7 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 4 vCPUs, derived from 2 physical cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The processor includes 32 MB of L3 cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 48 KB of L1d cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, including AVX512_VNNI and AVX512_BF16, alongside cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI.

This server is configured with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32.0 GB per vCPU. Storage is bundled with a 945 GB local disk, and the instance is Premium Storage capable. No GPUs are included in this configuration, and it comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in single-core operations, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression all ranking in the top 10%. Multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth, both cached small block and uncached large block, perform in the average tier. Database operations, Redis SET operations at 7.7 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput also align with average tier expectations. However, medium-model LLM inference prompt processing represents a performance bottleneck, registering in the bottom 25% at 16.53 tokens per second.

Given the high memory density relative to the vCPU count, this instance offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel compute scaling. The combination of 128.0 GB of RAM, a 945 GB local disk, and strong single-core performance makes this server highly suitable for relational databases, in-memory caching layers like Redis, data buffering, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
description: The Microsoft Azure E16-4ads_v7 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the Eadsv7 family, powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. Configured with 4 vCPUs and an extensive 128.0 GB of RAM, it provides an exceptionally high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32.0 GB per core. The server includes a 945 GB local disk and supports Premium Storage, though it lacks GPU resources. Benchmark results highlight top-tier single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression speeds, while multi-core operations, memory bandwidth, and database operations perform in the average tier. Conversely, medium-model LLM prompt processing is a relative bottleneck. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy, low-compute-density tasks, making it a strong fit for in-memory databases, caching systems, and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E16-4ads_v7 is an AMD EPYC-powered virtual machine featuring 4 vCPUs, 128 GB of RAM, and a 945 GB local disk, delivering top-tier single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E16-4ads_v7 is a memory-optimized VM with 4 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and a 945 GB local disk, ideal for database and caching workloads.
tagline: A memory-dense virtual machine featuring high-speed single-core AMD EPYC performance and local storage for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz
High memory density with 128.0 GB RAM across 4 vCPUs
Bundled 945 GB local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Well-suited for in-memory databases, caching, and memory-heavy workloads
